subject: Mantle Turing Test Hackathon 2026 Is Underway! Build AI Agents On-Chain ($100K in Prizes)
body:
[https://cdn.dorahacks.io/static/files/187287ebf6134204c341a364da7975f6.png]

[https://cdn.dorahacks.io/static/files/19de9a7c8071fb77b238c24446b90ee8.jpg]

Hi Builders,

Mantle’s Turing Test Hackathon 2026
[https://dorahacks.io/hackathon/mantleturingtesthackathon2026/detail?utm_source=new&utm_medium=dora&utm_campaign=0522]
is actively underway on DoraHacks, bringing together AI and Web3 builders and
product teams to test how autonomous agents perform in real on-chain
environments. 

Top-performing projects are competing across multiple AI-focused tracks for a
$100,000 total prize pool.

📅 Submission Deadline: June 15, 2026.

👉 Deploy Your AI Agent on Mantle 👈
[https://dorahacks.io/hackathon/mantleturingtesthackathon2026/detail?utm_source=new&utm_medium=dora&utm_campaign=0522]

💡 Online Mentor Clinic Workshops for Builders

Every Friday, 12:00 - 13:00 UTC: Mantle Mentor Clinic workshops and live
feedback sessions. The Clinic runs across four Fridays during the hackathon.

 · Register for Mentor Clinic > [https://forms.gle/dRLV3dZQP2QKtfCv6]

 · Full workshop details >
[https://x.com/mantle_official/status/2054908648238047637?s=46]

The right questions, direct feedback, and guidance from experienced builders can
significantly improve your project before final judging.

🏆 $100,000 Prize Pool

 · Grand Champion: $9,000

 · Track First Prizes: $51,000 total

 · Community Voting: $17,000 total

 · Best UI/UX Award: $3,000

 · Finalist & Deployment Awards: $20,000 total

Full prize details >
[https://dorahacks.io/hackathon/mantleturingtesthackathon2026/detail?utm_source=new&utm_medium=dora&utm_campaign=0522#prize-pool-breakdown]

Submission requirements >
[https://dorahacks.io/hackathon/mantleturingtesthackathon2026/detail?utm_source=new&utm_medium=dora&utm_campaign=0522#three-defining-features-of-the-turing-test-hackathon]

💬 Community & Support

 · Follow updates on Mantle Official X [https://x.com/Mantle_Official]

 · Join the Mantle Community Channel [https://t.me/mantlenetwork]

· Platform support:@dorahacksofficial [https://t.me/dorahacksofficial] on
Telegram

🌐 About Mantle

Mantle is a distribution layer connecting institutions and TradFi to on-chain
liquidity and real-world assets, with an ecosystem designed to support
large-scale adoption through credibility, liquidity, and institutional-grade
infrastructure.

This hackathon is co-hosted by Bybit, Byreal, and Blockchain for Good Alliance.
Backed by Tencent Cloud and a broad coalition of AI and Web3 ecosystem partners.



[https://ci3.googleusercontent.com/meips/ADKq_NYpeH826Kb71RefoWo-X3-M9L9zbbclN5HJ38QOQkwWtESwrnD318NmbPwQTdQsi6DmMqHoZafgMaI-ghwuwlmLUJiHTHLXax3qv9LXf_W_WfVMF-WhadY8-sliZHKAiw=s0-d-e1-ft#https://cdn.dorahacks.io/static/files/192c31fa95cb440e8b67b3444d38030d.png]

The Everlasting Hacker Movement

We've helped 40,000 open source projects and more than 400,000 open source
developers grow their projects and communities through DoraHacks.

subject: [Osmosis Community Hall] Summary
body:
A brief summary of [Osmosis Community Hall][1] since 2026-05-15 07:59:52 UTC

2 New Topics
3 New Users


  
  ### Popular Topics

    [Reduce Validator set to 70][2]

      This proposal reduces the Osmosis active validator set from 100 to 70. 
Motivation
This proposal follows Proposal 976, which reduced the validator set from 120 to 100. Continuing to reduce the active set incrementally contributes to more efficient network communication, reduced hardware demands on validators, and faster potential block finality, benefits that improve both user experience and the long-term scalability of the chain. 
Decentralization Impact
Decentralization is a trade-off in terms of performance, with smaller validator sets producing blocks faster, more reliably, and at a lower CPU cost. 

Validator distribution remains broad:

Top 33.3% of stake: 8 validators
Top 67%: 24 validators
Bottom 33.3%: 59 validators (plus 9 empty slots)



These figures demonstrate that trimming the bottom portion of the set can reduce technical overhead, which is determined by the number of validators present, without significantly affecting the number of validators required for each consensu...

    
    [Increase Smoothing Factor 14 to 28][3]

      This proposal increases the smoothing factor for staking rewards from 14 to 28 days, providing a more stable staking APR once the distribution wallet completes its initial accumulation period. 
Current Status
Osmosis staking rewards are distributed using a smoothing mechanism that averages staking reward distributions over a fixed number of days. 
The most recent change was in Proposal 996, where the parameter was increased to 14 days. 
As the APR display indicates the yearly return for stakers, it may be further smoothed incrementally as the distributing address accumulates. 
Proposed Change
When the smoothing factor increases from 14 to 28 days: 
The distribution wallet will need to accumulate an additional 14 days of staking rewards for staking rewards to return to the normal level. 
During this accumulation period, the displayed APR will appear lower as the wallet builds its balance. 
Once equilibrium is reached, the APR will stabilize and fluctuate less sharply each day. 
This cha...

    

  







[1]: https://forum.osmosis.zone/
[2]: https://forum.osmosis.zone/t/reduce-validator-set-to-70/4029
[3]: https://forum.osmosis.zone/t/increase-smoothing-factor-14-to-28/4030
[4]: https://forum.osmosis.zone/



  This summary is sent from [Osmosis Community Hall][4] when we haven't seen you in a while. Change [your email settings][5], or [click here][6] to unsubscribe.

[5]: https://forum.osmosis.zone/my/preferences/emails
[6]: https://forum.osmosis.zone/email/unsubscribe/9f987dd3b12b9cc5ebcf52aba49b42f62bc02efa065ab539d07b861ef2461005
